mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or median 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital spending growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median 60-day returns in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle confirms resilient corporate output despite higher rates, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in > 60% of tightening periods over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero predictive power with Rank IC equal to 0.00 over consecutive 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above threshold consistently precede positive 20-day market returns in > 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 20-day horizons with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signal weakening real estate investment and broader demand contraction, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines in late-cycle periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 65% of observed cases.
